280	    def _load_customization(self) -> None:
281	        from minima_harness.tui.customize import load_skills, load_templates
282	        from minima_harness.tui.mubit import available, get_skills
283	        from minima_harness.tui.theme import reload_file_themes
284	
285	        reload_file_themes(self.cwd)
286	        self._templates = load_templates(self.cwd)
287	        self._skills = load_skills(self.cwd)
288	        # Merge Mubit-stored skills (project-scoped) alongside local SKILL.md files.
289	        if available():
290	            for skill in get_skills(self.cwd):
291	                name = skill.get("name") or skill.get("function", {}).get("name", "")
292	                inst = (
293	                    skill.get("instructions")
294	                    or skill.get("description")
295	                    or skill.get("function", {}).get("description", "")
296	                )
297	                if name and inst and name not in self._skills:
298	                    self._skills[name] = f"# Mubit skill: {name}\n{inst}"
299	
300	    def _apply_theme(self) -> None:
301	        for bubble in self.query_one(ChatLog).query(MessageBubble):
302	            bubble.refresh_theme()
303	        self._refresh_footer()

305	    # ------------------------------------------------------------- extensions
306	    def _apply_extensions(self) -> None:
307	        """Merge extension tools/commands into the agent + registry (init + /reload)."""
308	        ext_tools = [t for ext in self._extensions for t in ext.tools]
309	        self.agent.state.tools = list(self._tools) + ext_tools
310	        for name in self._ext_cmd_names:
311	            self.commands.remove_command(name)
312	        self._ext_cmd_names = []
313	        for ext in self._extensions:
314	            for cname, cmd in ext.commands.items():
315	                self.commands.add_command(cmd)
316	                self._ext_cmd_names.append(cname)
317	
318	    async def _extension_fanout(self, event: Any) -> None:
319	        if isinstance(event, ToolExecutionStartEvent):
320	            key = "tool_start"
321	        elif isinstance(event, ToolExecutionEndEvent):
322	            key = "tool_end"
323	        elif isinstance(event, AgentEndEvent):
324	            key = "finish"
325	        elif isinstance(event, MessageUpdateEvent):
326	            key = "text"
327	        elif isinstance(event, TurnEndEvent):
328	            key = "turn"
329	        else:
330	            return
331	        for ext in self._extensions:
332	            for handler in ext.hooks.get(key, []):
333	                try:
334	                    result = handler(event)
335	                    if inspect.isawaitable(result):
336	                        await result
337	                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - an extension hook must not break the run
338	                    _log.warning("extension_hook_failed", exc_info=True)
339	
340	    def _default_footer_state(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
341	        # Pre-turn placeholder: Minima picks the model per turn, so show "auto" rather
342	        # than the offline default (gpt-4o-mini), which would be misleading.
343	        return {
344	            "model": "auto",
345	            "basis": "minima",
346	            "input_tokens": 0,
347	            "output_tokens": 0,
348	            "cache_read": 0,
349	            "cache_write": 0,
350	            "ctx_pct": 0.0,
351	        }

353	    # ------------------------------------------------------------- layout
354	    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
355	        yield Header()
356	        yield Static(id="banner")
357	        yield ChatLog(id="chatlog")
358	        yield OptionList(id="cmd-popup")
359	        yield Editor()
360	        yield StatusBar(id="status")
361	        yield TextualFooter()

363	    def get_key_display(self, binding: Binding) -> str:
364	        """Spell out ``ctrl+x`` in the footer instead of Textual's default ``^x`` caret.
365	
366	        Mirrors the stock implementation byte-for-byte except the ctrl modifier renders as a
367	        literal ``ctrl+`` prefix — other modifiers (shift/alt) and bare keys (esc, pgup) keep
368	        their normal display.
369	        """
370	        if binding.key_display:
371	            return binding.key_display
372	        modifiers, key = binding.parse_key()
373	        key = format_key(key)
374	        if "ctrl" in modifiers:
375	            modifiers.pop(modifiers.index("ctrl"))
376	            key = f"ctrl+{key}"
377	        return "+".join([*modifiers, key])
378	
379	    def on_mount(self) -> None:
380	        self.title = "Minima CLI"
381	        self.agent.subscribe(self.bridge)
382	        self.agent.subscribe(self._extension_fanout)
383	        self.bridge.bind(on_text=self._append_stream, on_thinking=self._on_thinking)
384	        self.query_one(Editor).prompt_history = self._history
385	        self.query_one(Editor).focus()
386	        self._refresh_footer()
387	        self._apply_effective_prompt()
388	        self.run_worker(self._show_welcome(), exclusive=True)

390	    def _apply_effective_prompt(self) -> None:
391	        """Recompute and apply the Mubit+local+session system prompt to the agent, with the
392	        active goal + open tasks appended so the model is re-anchored to the goal each turn."""
393	        base = effective_prompt(self.cwd, get_session_override(self.session))
394	        goal_block = self._goals.prompt_block()
395	        self.agent.state.system_prompt = f"{base}\n\n{goal_block}" if goal_block else base

397	    async def _apply_prompt_edit(self, result: dict) -> None:
398	        action, content = result["action"], result["content"]
399	        if action == "project":
400	            ok = mubit_set_prompt(content)
401	            msg = (
402	                "system prompt saved to Mubit (project, versioned)"
403	                if ok
404	                else "Mubit save failed — prompt unchanged"
405	            )
406	        else:
407	            set_session_override(self.session, content)
408	            msg = "session prompt override saved"
409	        self._apply_effective_prompt()

431	    def copy_to_clipboard(self, text: str) -> None:
432	        """Copy ``text`` to the clipboard. Textual's built-in copy (triggered by the in-app
433	        text selection + ⌘/Ctrl+C) emits *only* OSC 52, which macOS Terminal.app silently
434	        ignores — so a selection looked copied but wasn't. Also push to the OS clipboard tool
435	        (pbcopy/xclip/wl-copy) so selection-copy lands on the real clipboard everywhere, and
436	        through tmux/SSH. Run off the UI thread so a slow subprocess never stalls the app."""
437	        super().copy_to_clipboard(text)  # Textual: track _clipboard + emit OSC 52
438	        if not text:
439	            return
440	        try:
441	            self.run_worker(
442	                partial(_os_clipboard_copy, text),
443	                thread=True,
444	                group="clipboard",
445	                exclusive=True,
446	            )
447	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - copy must never crash the app
448	            _log.debug("clipboard_worker_failed", exc_info=True)
449	
450	    def _set_mouse_capture(self, enabled: bool) -> bool:
451	        """Turn mouse capture on/off live via the driver. Returns True on success. Mouse capture
452	        is what trades terminal-native selection for scroll-wheel + in-app selection, so this lets
453	        a user flip between the two without restarting."""
454	        driver = getattr(self, "_driver", None)
455	        if driver is None:
456	            return False
457	        try:
458	            if enabled:
459	                driver._enable_mouse_support()
460	            else:
461	                driver._disable_mouse_support()
462	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never crash on a terminal that can't toggle
463	            _log.debug("mouse_toggle_failed", exc_info=True)
464	            return False
465	        self._mouse_enabled = enabled
466	        return True

495	    async def _finalize_thinking(self) -> None:
496	        """Drop the per-turn thinking bubble if the model produced no thoughts; else keep it."""
497	        if self._thinking_bubble is None:
498	            return
499	        if not self._thinking_bubble.buffer.strip():
500	            await self._thinking_bubble.remove()
501	        else:
502	            self._thinking_bubble.flush()
503	        self._thinking_bubble = None
504	
505	    async def _emit_goal_cost_line(self, routing: Any) -> None:
506	        """Attribute this turn's realized cost to the active goal and show spent/projected/budget.
507	
508	        The one thing no other agent does: frame the goal as a budget. spent = realized cost since
509	        the goal started; projected = linear extrapolation from task progress; budget warns (never
510	        blocks) when exceeded."""
511	        if routing is None or not self._goals.active or self._goals.goal is None:
512	            return
513	        meter = self.agent.meter
514	        if meter is None or not meter.rows:
515	            return
516	        row = meter.rows[-1]
517	        # Tasks the model flipped to completed THIS turn get the cost split across them (covers
518	        # the common case: model plans, works, then marks several done with no in_progress step).
519	        before: set[str] = getattr(self, "_goal_completed_before", set())
520	        newly_completed = [tid for tid in self._goals.completed_ids() if tid not in before]
521	        self._goals.record_turn_cost(row.actual_cost_usd, row.est_cost_usd, newly_completed)
522	        g = self._goals.goal
523	        spent = g.spent_usd()
524	        parts = [f"spent ${spent:.4f}"]
525	        proj = g.projected_total_usd()
526	        if proj is not None:
527	            parts.append(f"~${proj:.4f} projected")
528	        over = False
529	        if g.budget_usd:
530	            pct = (100.0 * spent / g.budget_usd) if g.budget_usd > 0 else 0.0
531	            over = spent > g.budget_usd
532	            parts.append(f"budget ${g.budget_usd:.4f} ({pct:.0f}%)")
533	        await self.query_one(ChatLog).add_system(
534	            "   └ ledger · " + " · ".join(parts), color="red" if over else None
535	        )
536	
537	    async def _check_escalate(self, routing: Any, task_text: str) -> None:
538	        """Judge the output and suggest escalating if quality < threshold."""
539	        chatlog = self.query_one(ChatLog)
540	        last = self.agent._last_assistant()
541	        if last is None or not last.text.strip():
542	            return
543	        try:
544	            quality = await self.agent.judge.grade(task_text, last.text)
545	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
546	            return
547	        if quality is not None and quality < self._escalate_threshold:
548	            stronger = max(
549	                routing.ranked,
550	                key=lambda r: r.predicted_success,
551	                default=None,
552	            )
553	            if stronger and stronger.model_id != routing.chosen_model_id:
554	                await chatlog.add_system(
555	                    f"↗ quality {quality:.2f} < {self._escalate_threshold} — "
556	                    f"consider {stronger.model_id} ({stronger.predicted_success:.0%} success). "
557	                    f"/model {stronger.model_id} to pin it."
558	                )
559	            elif quality is not None:
560	                await chatlog.add_system(
561	                    f"quality {quality:.2f} < {self._escalate_threshold} "
562	                    f"(already on the strongest candidate)"
563	                )
564	
565	    async def _emit_cost_line(self) -> None:
566	        """Close the loop visibly: est (from recommend) -> actual (from feedback) per turn."""
567	        meter = self.agent.meter
568	        if meter is None or not meter.rows:
569	            return
570	        r = meter.rows[-1]
571	        parts = [f"est ${r.est_cost_usd:.4f} → actual ${r.actual_cost_usd:.4f}"]
572	        if r.baseline_cost_usd is not None:
573	            save = r.baseline_cost_usd - r.actual_cost_usd
574	            pct = (100.0 * save / r.baseline_cost_usd) if r.baseline_cost_usd > 0 else 0.0
575	            verb = "saved" if save >= 0 else "over"
576	            parts.append(f"{verb} ${abs(save):.4f} ({abs(pct):.0f}%) vs baseline")
577	        await self.query_one(ChatLog).add_system("   └ " + " · ".join(parts))
578	
579	    async def _route_hook(self, routing: Any, task_text: str) -> Any:
580	        """before_route hook: always emits a rationale line; shows confirm panel when on."""
581	        chatlog = self.query_one(ChatLog)
582	        reason = ""
583	        if routing is not None:
584	            chosen = routing.chosen_model_id or routing.model.id
585	            chosen_r = _chosen_ranking(routing)
586	            level, color = _confidence_band(routing)
587	            extra = _reasoner_note(routing)
588	            cost = _fmt_cost_range(
589	                routing.est_cost_usd, routing.est_cost_low, routing.est_cost_high
590	            )
591	            lat = _fmt_latency(chosen_r.est_latency_ms if chosen_r else None)
592	            line = (
593	                f"● routed to {chosen} · {routing.decision_basis} · {cost} · {lat} "
594	                f"· conf {routing.confidence:.0%} ({level}){extra}"
595	            )
596	            await chatlog.add_system(line, color=color)
597	            reason = _routing_reason(routing)
598	            if reason:
599	                await chatlog.add_system(f"   └ {reason}")  # cost/speed/predictability story
600	        if self._route_mode != "confirm" or routing is None:
601	            return None  # accept as-is
602	        result = await self.push_screen(RoutingConfirm(routing, reason), wait_for_dismiss=True)
603	        if result is None or result.get("action") == "cancel":
604	            routing.recommendation_id = None  # veto feedback
605	            return routing
606	        chosen_id = result.get("model_id")
607	        action = result.get("action")
608	        if chosen_id:
609	            provider = next((r.provider for r in routing.ranked if r.model_id == chosen_id), "")
610	            model = self.agent.router.mapping._resolve(provider, chosen_id)  # noqa: SLF001
611	            if model is not None:
612	                routing.model = model
613	                routing.chosen_model_id = chosen_id
614	            else:
615	                # The pick isn't a model the harness can actually call (id unknown to the
616	                # registry) — say so instead of silently running the originally-routed model.
617	                await chatlog.add_error(
618	                    f"can't switch to {chosen_id} — not a registered model; "
619	                    f"running {routing.chosen_model_id or routing.model.id}"
620	                )
621	        if action == "pin" and chosen_id:
622	            self.config.candidates = [chosen_id]
623	        return routing
624	
625	    def _tool_preview(self, name: str, args: Any) -> str:
626	        """What a sensitive tool call will do, for the permission modal: a diff for write/edit,
627	        the command for bash, else a compact summary."""
628	        if name in _MUTATING_TOOLS:
629	            from minima_harness.tui.diff import render_tool_diff
630	
631	            return render_tool_diff(name, args)
632	        if name == "bash":
633	            return f"$ {getattr(args, 'command', '') or ''}"
634	        return _format_tool_call(name, args)
635	
636	    async def _tool_hook(self, ctx: Any) -> Any:
637	        """before_tool_call hook: ask the user to approve sensitive ops (write/edit/bash) before
638	        they run — Claude-Code-style. Approval is needed when permission-asking is on and the
639	        tool isn't already always-allowed, OR when /edits forces a diff review for edits.
640	
641	        A rejected call is blocked (the model sees the rejection) AND recorded as a ground-truth
642	        negative outcome fed back to Minima.
643	        """
644	        from minima_harness.agent.tools import BeforeToolCallResult
645	
646	        name = ctx.tool_call.name
647	        forced = self._confirm_edits and name in _MUTATING_TOOLS
648	        gated = self._ask_permission and name in _SENSITIVE_TOOLS and name not in self._allow_always
649	        if not (forced or gated):
650	            return None
651	        target = getattr(ctx.args, "path", "") or ""
652	        preview = self._tool_preview(name, ctx.args)
653	        result = await self.push_screen(
654	            PermissionRequest(name, preview, target), wait_for_dismiss=True
655	        )
656	        action = (result or {}).get("action", "reject")
657	        if action == "always":
658	            self._allow_always.add(name)  # don't ask again for this tool this session
659	            return None
660	        if action == "approve":
661	            return None
662	        self.agent.record_tool_rejection()
663	        await self.query_one(ChatLog).add_error(f"rejected {name} {target}".rstrip())
664	        return BeforeToolCallResult(
665	            block=True,
666	            reason="The user rejected this tool call. Do not retry it verbatim — propose a "
667	            "different approach or ask what they want.",
668	        )
669	
670	    # ------------------------------------------------------------- input
671	    async def on_editor_submitted(self, event: Editor.Submitted) -> None:
672	        text = event.text
673	        self.query_one("#cmd-popup", OptionList).set_class(False, "visible")
674	        if text.strip():
675	            self._history.add(text)
676	            append_history(self.cwd, text)
677	        if self.agent.state.is_streaming:
678	            # Enter while running = steering (delivered after the current tool batch).
679	            self.agent.steer(text)
680	            self.query_one(Editor).text = ""
681	            await self.query_one(ChatLog).add_system(f"↳ (steering) {text}")
682	            return
683	        self.query_one(Editor).text = ""
684	        parsed = parse_submission(text)
685	        kind = parsed["kind"]
686	        if kind == "command":
687	            await self._dispatch_command(parsed["name"], parsed["args"])
688	            self._refresh_footer()
689	            return
690	        self.run_worker(self._run_submission(parsed), exclusive=True, name="turn")
691	
692	    async def on_editor_follow_up(self, event: Editor.FollowUp) -> None:
693	        text = event.text
694	        if not text.strip():
695	            return
696	        self.agent.follow_up(text)
697	        self.query_one(Editor).text = ""
698	        await self.query_one(ChatLog).add_system(f"↳ (follow-up) {text}")
699	
700	    # ------------------------------------------------------------- command popup
701	    def on_text_area_changed(self, event: TextArea.Changed) -> None:
702	        text = event.text_area.text
703	        popup = self.query_one("#cmd-popup", OptionList)
704	        frag = text[1:] if text.startswith("/") else ""
705	        if text.startswith("/") and " " not in frag:
706	            matches = [c for c in self.commands.all() if not frag or c.name.startswith(frag)]
707	            if matches:
708	                popup.clear_options()
709	                for c in matches:
710	                    label = f"/{c.name}  {c.description}".rstrip()
711	                    popup.add_option(Option(label, id=c.name))
712	                popup.set_class(True, "visible")
713	            else:
714	                popup.set_class(False, "visible")
715	        else:
716	            popup.set_class(False, "visible")
717	
718	    def on_editor_complete_requested(self, event: Editor.CompleteRequested) -> None:
719	        text = event.text
720	        if not text.startswith("/") or " " in text[1:]:
721	            return
722	        frag = text[1:]
723	        matches = [c for c in self.commands.all() if not frag or c.name.startswith(frag)]
724	        if not matches:
725	            return
726	        ed = self.query_one(Editor)
727	        ed.text = f"/{matches[0].name} "
728	        ed.move_cursor((0, len(ed.text)))
729	        self.query_one("#cmd-popup", OptionList).set_class(False, "visible")
730	
731	    def on_editor_cycle_thinking(self, event: Editor.CycleThinking) -> None:
732	        levels = ("off", "low", "medium", "high")
733	        cur = self.agent.state.thinking_level
734	        nxt = levels[(levels.index(cur) + 1) % len(levels)] if cur in levels else "low"
735	        self.agent.state.thinking_level = nxt  # type: ignore[assignment]
736	        self._refresh_footer()  # thinking level lives in the footer now, not the warning banner
737	
738	    async def _run_submission(self, parsed: dict) -> None:
739	        try:
740	            if parsed["kind"] == "bash":
741	                output = await run_bash(parsed["command"])
742	                if parsed["feed"]:
743	                    await self.run_turn(output)
744	                else:
745	                    await self.query_one(ChatLog).add_system(f"$ {parsed['command']}\n{output}")
746	            elif parsed["kind"] == "message":
747	                await self.run_turn(parsed["text"])
748	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - a bad turn must not kill the app
749	            _log.warning("turn_failed", exc_info=True)
750	            await self.query_one(ChatLog).add_error("turn failed (see logs)")
751	
752	    # ------------------------------------------------------------- a turn
753	    async def run_turn(self, text: str) -> None:
754	        chatlog = self.query_one(ChatLog)
755	        self._dismiss_welcome()  # first prompt: drop the splash, let the conversation flow top-down
756	        self._apply_effective_prompt()  # re-anchor the goal/tasks into the system prompt
757	        await chatlog.add_user(text)
758	        self.session.append(EntryType.USER, {"text": text})
759	        if self._cache_enabled:
760	            hit = self._cache.get(text)
761	            if hit is not None:
762	                await self._serve_cache_hit(text, hit)
763	                return
764	        # A live "thinking" bubble (above the answer) when /thoughts is on; dropped if empty.
765	        self._thinking_bubble = await chatlog.add_thinking_stream() if self._show_thinking else None
766	        self._stream_bubble = await chatlog.add_assistant_stream()
767	        self._set_spinner_tip()
768	        self._set_state("routing")
769	        routing = None
770	        resp_text = ""
771	        # Goal-conditioned routing: an active goal supplies task_type + a goal tag so the whole
772	        # goal routes coherently and clusters in Minima's memory.
773	        g_type, g_tags = (None, None)
774	        self._goal_completed_before = self._goals.completed_ids()  # for per-task cost attribution
775	        if self._goals.active and self._goals.goal is not None:
776	            g_type, g_tags = self._goals.goal.routing_signals()
777	        try:
778	            routing = await self.agent.prompt(text, task_type=g_type, tags=g_tags)
779	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
780	            self._set_state("idle")
781	            await self._finalize_thinking()
782	            if self._stream_bubble is not None:
783	                await self._stream_bubble.remove()  # never leave an empty bubble behind
784	                self._stream_bubble = None
785	            await chatlog.add_error(str(exc))
786	            self._set_banner(str(exc))
787	            return
788	        await self._finalize_thinking()
789	        await self._render_tools_post_turn()
790	        # A provider call that failed (bad/missing key, 404, rate limit, network) is swallowed
791	        # into an empty assistant (stop_reason="error"); the agent classifies it as _last_error.
792	        # Surface that reason instead of leaving a silent blank bubble.
793	        turn_error = getattr(self.agent, "_last_error", None)
794	        if self._stream_bubble is not None:
795	            if turn_error and not self._stream_bubble.buffer.strip():
796	                await self._stream_bubble.remove()  # no output at all → drop the blank bubble
797	            else:
798	                self._stream_bubble.render_markdown()
799	                resp_text = self._stream_bubble.buffer
800	                _last = self.agent._last_assistant()
801	                _usage = _last.usage if _last is not None else None
802	                self.session.append(
803	                    EntryType.ASSISTANT,
804	                    {
805	                        "text": self._stream_bubble.buffer,
806	                        "model": routing.chosen_model_id if routing else None,
807	                        "in_tokens": _usage.input if _usage else 0,
808	                        "out_tokens": _usage.output if _usage else 0,
809	                        "cost": _usage.cost.total if _usage else 0.0,
810	                        # est cost (+ band) so predictability (est-vs-actual) is computable later.
811	                        "est_cost": routing.est_cost_usd if routing else 0.0,
812	                        "est_cost_low": routing.est_cost_low if routing else None,
813	                        "est_cost_high": routing.est_cost_high if routing else None,
814	                    },
815	                )
816	            self._stream_bubble = None
817	        if turn_error:
818	            # The *model call* failed (routing succeeded) — surface it as a model error, NOT
819	            # the "routing offline … /reconnect to retry Minima" banner (reconnecting won't fix
820	            # a bad provider key / quota / 404). The message already names the next step.
821	            await chatlog.add_error(turn_error)
822	            # Show the provider's RAW words too (muted) — an ambiguous 403/429 ("permission, or
823	            # no quota") is only diagnosable from the provider's exact reason.
824	            raw = getattr(self.agent, "_last_error_raw", None)
825	            if raw and raw.strip() and raw.strip() not in turn_error:
826	                await chatlog.add_system(f"   └ provider said: {_snippet(raw, 300)}")
827	            self._set_model_error_banner(turn_error)
828	            self._scroll_bottom()
829	            self._refresh_footer()
830	            self._set_state("idle")
831	            return
832	        # If a dead-key provider was auto-rerouted around, say so (the turn otherwise looks like a
833	        # normal success on the fallback model — the user should know their key was rejected).
834	        reroute = getattr(self.agent, "_reroute_note", None)
835	        if reroute and resp_text.strip():
836	            model = routing.chosen_model_id if routing else "an available model"
837	            await chatlog.add_system(f"⚠ {reroute} · re-routed to {model}", color="yellow")
838	        self._scroll_bottom()
839	        await self._emit_cost_line()
840	        await self._emit_goal_cost_line(routing)
841	        if self._escalate and routing is not None:
842	            await self._check_escalate(routing, text)
843	        # Cache a clean, successful answer so a near-duplicate prompt is free next time.
844	        if self._cache_enabled and routing is not None and resp_text:
845	            self._cache.put(text, resp_text)
846	        self._after_turn(routing)
847	
848	    async def _serve_cache_hit(self, text: str, hit: Any) -> None:
849	        """Return a cached response: render it, log a $0 CostMeter row, skip Minima entirely."""
850	        chatlog = self.query_one(ChatLog)
851	        bubble = await chatlog.add_assistant_stream()
852	        bubble.set_text(hit.response)
853	        bubble.render_markdown()
854	        self.session.append(
855	            EntryType.ASSISTANT,
856	            {
857	                "text": hit.response,
858	                "model": "(cache)",
859	                "in_tokens": 0,
860	                "out_tokens": 0,
861	                "cost": 0.0,
862	            },
863	        )
864	        await chatlog.add_system(
865	            f"⚡ cache hit (similarity {hit.similarity:.2f}) · $0.0000", color="green"
866	        )
867	        meter = self.agent.meter
868	        if meter is not None:
869	            meter.rows.append(
870	                CostRow(
871	                    label=text.splitlines()[0][:48] if text.strip() else "(empty)",
872	                    model="(cache)",
873	                    decision_basis="cache",
874	                    est_cost_usd=0.0,
875	                    actual_cost_usd=0.0,
876	                    baseline_cost_usd=None,
877	                    quality=None,
878	                    outcome="success",
879	                )
880	            )
881	        self._scroll_bottom()
882	        self._refresh_footer()
883	        self._set_state("idle")
884	
885	    async def _render_tools_post_turn(self) -> None:
886	        chatlog = self.query_one(ChatLog)
887	        for msg in self.agent.state.messages[self._rendered_msgs :]:
888	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
889	                for call in msg.tool_calls:
890	                    await chatlog.add_tool(call.name, _format_tool_call(call.name, call.arguments))
891	            elif msg.role == "toolResult":
892	                # Errors (incl. permission/sandbox failures) get more room + a prominent ✗ so a
893	                # failed tool is never an easy-to-miss faint line.
894	                limit = 400 if msg.is_error else 120
895	                await chatlog.add_tool_result(_snippet(msg.text, limit), msg.is_error)
896	        self._rendered_msgs = len(self.agent.state.messages)
897	
898	    async def _load_session(self, store: SessionStore) -> None:
899	        """Switch the active session and rebuild the agent context + transcript from it."""
900	        self.session = store
901	        self._goals.load(store)  # restore the session's goal/task list
902	        chatlog = self.query_one(ChatLog)
903	        await chatlog.remove_children()
904	        chatlog.remove_class("empty")
905	        self._rendered_msgs = 0
906	        msgs: list = []
907	        for entry in store.entries:
908	            txt = entry.payload.get("text", "")
909	            if entry.type == EntryType.USER:
910	                await chatlog.add_user(txt)
911	                msgs.append(Message(role="user", content=txt))
912	            elif entry.type == EntryType.ASSISTANT:
913	                bubble = await chatlog.add_assistant_stream()
914	                bubble.set_text(txt)
915	                bubble.render_markdown()
916	                msgs.append(AssistantMessage(role="assistant", content=[TextContent(text=txt)]))
917	        self.agent.state.messages = msgs
918	        self._rendered_msgs = len(msgs)
919	        label = store.display_name or (store.path.stem if store.path else "ephemeral")
920	        await chatlog.add_system(f"resumed {label} ({len(msgs)} msg(s) in context)")
921	        self._refresh_footer()
922	
923	    def _after_turn(self, routing: Any) -> None:
924	        if routing is None:
925	            # Offline fallback. A retryable cause (unreachable/timeout) gets the
926	            # "routing offline … /reconnect" framing; a config/auth cause (no/invalid key)
927	            # gets the actionable banner instead — /reconnect alone wouldn't fix it.
928	            self._routing_offline = True
929	            reason = getattr(self.agent, "_offline_reason", None) or "Minima unreachable"
930	            retryable = getattr(self.agent, "_offline_retryable", True)
931	            model = self.agent.state.model.id if self.agent.state.model else "default model"
932	            self._footer_state["model"] = model
933	            self._footer_state["basis"] = "offline"
934	            if retryable:
935	                self._set_banner(f"{reason} — ran {model} unrouted")
936	            else:
937	                self._set_config_banner(f"{reason} (ran {model} unrouted)")
938	        else:
939	            # Routing SUCCEEDED. Surface only actionable, not-already-inline conditions —
940	            # never the "routing offline/reconnect" framing (that's a false alarm here).
941	            self._footer_state = self._routing_footer_state(routing)
942	            notices = _banner_warnings(routing.warnings)
943	            if notices:
944	                self._set_notice("; ".join(notices[:2]))
945	            elif self._footer_state["ctx_pct"] > 80:
946	                self._set_notice("context near limit — /compact to free space")
947	            else:
948	                self.query_one("#banner", Static).update(Text(""))
949	        # Persist any goal/task changes the model made via the `tasks` tool this turn.
950	        self._goals.save(self.session)
951	        self._refresh_footer()
952	        self._set_state("idle")
953	
954	    def _routing_footer_state(self, routing: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
955	        last = self.agent._last_assistant()
956	        usage = last.usage if last is not None else None
957	        ctx = 0.0
958	        if usage is not None and routing.model.context_window:
959	            ctx = 100.0 * usage.input / max(1, routing.model.context_window)
960	        return {
961	            "model": routing.chosen_model_id or routing.model.id,
962	            "basis": routing.decision_basis,
963	            "input_tokens": usage.input if usage else 0,
964	            "output_tokens": usage.output if usage else 0,
965	            "cache_read": usage.cache_read if usage else 0,
966	            "cache_write": usage.cache_write if usage else 0,
967	            "ctx_pct": ctx,
968	        }
969

992	    def _goal_footer(self) -> str:
993	        """`N/M` progress for the active goal (empty when none) — shown in the footer."""

1002	            self.session.path.stem if self.session.path else "ephemeral"
1003	        )

1007	            cwd=str(self.cwd),
1008	            session_id=session_label,

1010	            basis=self._footer_state["basis"],
1011	            meter=meter,
1012	            input_tokens=self._footer_state["input_tokens"],
1013	            output_tokens=self._footer_state["output_tokens"],
1014	            cache_read=self._footer_state["cache_read"],
1015	            cache_write=self._footer_state["cache_write"],
1016	            ctx_pct=self._footer_state["ctx_pct"],
1017	            routing_offline=self._routing_offline,
1018	            route_mode=self._route_mode,
1019	            thinking_level=str(self.agent.state.thinking_level),
1020	            goal=self._goal_footer(),
1021	        )
1022	        self.sub_title = ""
1023	        try:
1024	            self.query_one(StatusBar).set_idle_text(footer)  # rich Text: keep per-segment colour
1025	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - not mounted yet during early init

1065	        async def _model(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:
1066	            from minima_harness.ai import all_models
1067	            from minima_harness.ai.provider_catalog import runnable_candidates
1068	            from minima_harness.minima.config import DEFAULT_CANDIDATES
1069	
1070	            def _unpin() -> None:
1071	                # Release any pin: restore the full runnable candidate pool so Minima routes.
1072	                app.config.candidates = runnable_candidates(list(DEFAULT_CANDIDATES))
1073	                app.config.pinned = False
1074	                app._footer_state["model"] = "auto"
1075	                app._footer_state["basis"] = "minima"
1076	                app._clear_banner()  # a prior model's error/offline banner no longer applies
1077	                app._refresh_footer()
1078	
1079	            # `/model auto` (or unpin/clear) releases the pin without opening the picker.
1080	            if args.strip().lower() in ("auto", "unpin", "clear"):
1081	                _unpin()
1082	                await app.query_one(ChatLog).add_system("model: auto — Minima routes each turn")
1083	                return
1084	
1085	            # Offer the union of routing candidates + every registered model (candidates first,
1086	            # deduped) so a user can pin ANY provider's model — e.g. a Groq/DeepSeek model that
1087	            # isn't in the default routing pool. Pinning sets candidates=[chosen].
1088	            cands = list(app.config.candidates or [])
1089	            cands = list(dict.fromkeys(cands + [m.id for m in all_models()]))
1090	            providers = {m.id: m.provider for m in all_models()}
1091	            active = app._footer_state.get("model")
1092	            basis = app._footer_state.get("basis")
1093	            # Pinned iff the config holds exactly one candidate (check the CONFIG, not the
1094	            # union `cands` above which is always >1 — the old check could never detect a pin).
1095	            pinned = app.config.candidates[0] if len(app.config.candidates or []) == 1 else None
1096	
1097	            def _picked(chosen: str | None) -> None:
1098	                if not chosen:
1099	                    return
1100	                if chosen == ModelPicker.AUTO:
1101	                    _unpin()  # explicit "auto" entry: unpin back to Minima routing
1102	                    return
1103	                app.config.candidates = [chosen]  # pin → run this model directly (bypass Minima)
1104	                app.config.pinned = True
1105	                app._footer_state["model"] = chosen
1106	                app._footer_state["basis"] = "pinned"
1107	                # Clear any banner from the previous model — switching to `chosen` makes a
1108	                # prior model's "access denied"/offline banner stale and misleading.
1109	                app._clear_banner()
1110	                app._refresh_footer()  # reflect the pin immediately
1111	
1112	            app.push_screen(
1113	                ModelPicker(
1114	                    cands,
1115	                    active=active,
1116	                    basis=basis,
1117	                    pinned=pinned,
1118	                    providers=providers,
1119	                ),
1120	                callback=_picked,
1121	            )
1122	
1123	        async def _reconnect(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:
1124	            # Rebuild the Minima client from the current env so a key/URL set via /config (or
1125	            # exported since launch) actually takes effect — the old client's auth header was
1126	            # fixed at build time, which is why a plain banner-clear wasn't enough before.
1127	            await app.agent.reconnect()
1128	            app._routing_offline = False
1129	            app.query_one("#banner", Static).update(Text(""))
1130	            if (app.agent.config.minima_api_key or "").strip():
1131	                msg = "reconnected (next turn routes via Minima)"
1132	            else:
1133	                msg = (
1134	                    "reconnected — but no Mubit API key set, so routing stays offline; "
1135	                    "add MUBIT_API_KEY via /config"
1136	                )
1137	            await app.query_one(ChatLog).add_system(msg)
1138

1170	        async def _clone(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:

1173	                return

1177	            await app.query_one(ChatLog).add_system(f"cloned to {dest.stem}")

1179	        async def _resume(app: HarnessApp, args: str) -> None:
1180	            if args.strip():
1181	                try:
1182	                    store = SessionManager().open(app.cwd, session_id=args.strip())
1183	                except FileNotFoundError as exc:
1184	                    await app.query_one(ChatLog).add_error(str(exc))
1185	                    return
1186	                await app._load_session(store)
1187	                return
1188	            summaries = SessionManager().list_sessions(app.cwd)
1189	
1190	            def _picked(chosen: str | None) -> None:
1191	                if chosen:
1192	                    store = SessionStore.file_backed(Path(chosen))
1193	                    app.run_worker(app._load_session(store), exclusive=True)
1194	
1195	            app.push_screen(SessionPicker(summaries), callback=_picked)

1	"""Agent tools and execution hooks — port of PI's ``AgentTool`` + before/afterToolCall.
2	
3	Tools declare parameters as a pydantic model (the TypeBox analogue); ``execute`` is an
4	async callable ``(tool_call_id, params, signal, on_update) -> ToolResult``. Validation
5	errors and thrown exceptions become tool-error results fed back to the model so it can
6	retry (matching PI).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable
12	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
13	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Literal
14	
15	from pydantic import BaseModel
16	
17	from minima_harness.ai.types import ContentBlock, TextContent, ToolCall
18	
19	if TYPE_CHECKING:
20	    from minima_harness.agent.state import AgentState
21	
22	ToolExecutionMode = Literal["parallel", "sequential"]
23	ThinkingLevel = Literal["off", "minimal", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"]
24	
25	# on_update(partial_result) -> None; called mid-execution for streaming progress.
26	ToolUpdate = Callable[[Any], None]
27	ToolExecute = Callable[[str, BaseModel, object | None, ToolUpdate | None], Awaitable["ToolResult"]]
28	
29	
30	@dataclass(slots=True)
31	class ToolResult:
32	    """What a tool returns. ``content`` goes to the model; ``details`` are app-facing."""
33	
34	    content: list[ContentBlock]
35	    details: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
36	    # Hint to skip the automatic follow-up LLM call. Only honoured when every finalized
37	    # tool result in the batch also sets terminate=True.
38	    terminate: bool = False
39	
40	
41	@dataclass(slots=True)
42	class AgentTool:
43	    name: str
44	    description: str
45	    parameters: type[BaseModel]
46	    execute: ToolExecute
47	    execution_mode: ToolExecutionMode | None = None
48	    label: str = ""
49	
50	
51	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	# Hook types
53	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	
55	
56	@dataclass(slots=True)
57	class BeforeToolCallContext:
58	    tool_call: ToolCall
59	    args: BaseModel  # validated params
60	    context: AgentState
61	
62	
63	@dataclass(slots=True)
64	class BeforeToolCallResult:
65	    block: bool = False
66	    reason: str = ""
67	
68	
69	@dataclass(slots=True)
70	class AfterToolCallContext:
71	    tool_call: ToolCall
72	    result: ToolResult
73	    is_error: bool
74	    context: AgentState
75	
76	
77	@dataclass(slots=True)
78	class AfterToolCallResult:
79	    terminate: bool = False
80	    details: dict[str, Any] | None = None
81	    content: list[ContentBlock] | None = None
82	
83	
84	BeforeToolCall = Callable[[BeforeToolCallContext], Awaitable[BeforeToolCallResult | None]]
85	AfterToolCall = Callable[[AfterToolCallContext], Awaitable[AfterToolCallResult | None]]
86	
87	
88	def find_agent_tool(tools: list[AgentTool], name: str) -> AgentTool | None:
89	    for t in tools:
90	        if t.name == name:
91	            return t
92	    return None
93	
94	
95	def error_result(message: str) -> ToolResult:
96	    """A standard error tool result (single text block)."""
97	    return ToolResult(content=[TextContent(text=message)])

143	async def agent_loop_continue(state: AgentState, config: AgentLoopConfig) -> AsyncIterator[Any]:
144	    """Resume from existing context (last message must be user or toolResult)."""
145	    if state.messages:
146	        last = state.messages[-1]
147	        if last.role == "assistant":
148	            raise ValueError(
149	                "agent_loop_continue requires the last message to be user or toolResult"
150	            )

161	def _drop_failed_calls(messages: list[Message]) -> list[Message]:
162	    """Never send a failed call's assistant to a provider.
163	
164	    A provider error is swallowed into an assistant with ``stop_reason == "error"`` and
165	    (usually) empty content, which the loop appends to history before breaking. Left in the
166	    context it makes the *next* request invalid — e.g. Anthropic rejects an empty text block
167	    with ``400 "messages: text content blocks must be non-empty"`` — so a single provider
168	    hiccup would wedge the whole session. Filtering here protects every consumer of the loop
169	    (not just :class:`MinimaAgent`) and resumed sessions alike.
170	    """
171	    return [m for m in messages if getattr(m, "stop_reason", None) != "error"]
172	
173	
174	async def _prepare_messages(state: AgentState, config: AgentLoopConfig) -> list[Message]:
175	    messages = _drop_failed_calls(state.messages)
176	    if config.transform_context is not None:
177	        messages = await config.transform_context(messages, None)
178	    return config.convert_to_llm(messages)

181	def _stream_options(config: AgentLoopConfig) -> dict[str, Any]:
182	    opts: dict[str, Any] = dict(config.stream_options or {})
183	    opts["thinking"] = config.thinking_level != "off"
184	    if config.thinking_level != "off":

186	        if budget is not None:
187	            opts["thinking_budget"] = budget
188	    if config.session_id:
189	        opts["session_id"] = config.session_id
190	    return opts

636	    async def _tool_hook(self, ctx: Any) -> Any:
637	        """before_tool_call hook: ask the user to approve sensitive ops (write/edit/bash) before
638	        they run — Claude-Code-style. Approval is needed when permission-asking is on and the
639	        tool isn't already always-allowed, OR when /edits forces a diff review for edits.
640	
641	        A rejected call is blocked (the model sees the rejection) AND recorded as a ground-truth
642	        negative outcome fed back to Minima.
643	        """
644	        from minima_harness.agent.tools import BeforeToolCallResult
645	
646	        name = ctx.tool_call.name
647	        forced = self._confirm_edits and name in _MUTATING_TOOLS
648	        gated = self._ask_permission and name in _SENSITIVE_TOOLS and name not in self._allow_always
649	        if not (forced or gated):
650	            return None
651	        target = getattr(ctx.args, "path", "") or ""
652	        preview = self._tool_preview(name, ctx.args)
653	        result = await self.push_screen(
654	            PermissionRequest(name, preview, target), wait_for_dismiss=True
655	        )
656	        action = (result or {}).get("action", "reject")
657	        if action == "always":
658	            self._allow_always.add(name)  # don't ask again for this tool this session
659	            return None
660	        if action == "approve":
661	            return None
662	        self.agent.record_tool_rejection()
663	        await self.query_one(ChatLog).add_error(f"rejected {name} {target}".rstrip())
664	        return BeforeToolCallResult(
665	            block=True,
666	            reason="The user rejected this tool call. Do not retry it verbatim — propose a "
667	            "different approach or ask what they want.",
668	        )

494	class ConfigOverlay(ModalScreen[dict | None]):
495	    """Edit stored credentials, grouped into sections. Ctrl+S saves, Esc cancels.
496	
497	    Returns ``{key: value}`` for fields that were changed (already persisted to the store),
498	    or ``None`` on cancel. Secret inputs are password-masked and show the masked *current*
499	    value as a placeholder — the real secret is never pre-filled into an editable field.
500	    Leaving a field blank keeps its current value.
501	    """
502	
503	    BINDINGS = [
504	        Binding("ctrl+s", "save", "Save", priority=True),
505	        ("escape", "cancel"),
506	    ]
507	
508	    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
509	        backend = config_store.backend_name()
510	        with Vertical(id="config-card"):
511	            yield Static(
512	                Text("Enter your keys — blank keeps the current value. Any one provider works."),
513	                id="config-hint",
514	            )
515	            with VerticalScroll(id="config-body"):
516	                for section in config_store.SECTIONS:
517	                    yield Static(Text(section.title), classes="cfg-section")
518	                    yield Static(Text(section.note), classes="cfg-note")
519	                    for f in section.fields:
520	                        cur = config_store.get(f.key) or ""
521	                        if cur:
522	                            placeholder = config_store.mask(cur) if f.secret else cur
523	                        else:
524	                            placeholder = f.default or "(unset)"
525	                        tag = "   optional" if f.optional else ""
526	                        yield Static(Text(f"{f.key}{tag}"), classes="cfg-key")
527	                        yield Input(placeholder=placeholder, password=[REDACTED], id=f"cfg-{f.key}")
528	                yield Button("Save", id="cfg-save", variant="primary")
529	            # Always-visible footer (outside the scroll) so the save affordance never
530	            # scrolls out of sight while filling lower fields.
531	            yield Static(
532	                Text(f"Enter ▸ next field (lands on Save) · Ctrl+S ▸ save · Esc ▸ cancel  ·  "

535	            )

[tool_result] toolu_01Uzegd4eDpzUHrKzseh2i9W
1	"""Agent tools and execution hooks — port of PI's ``AgentTool`` + before/afterToolCall.
2	
3	Tools declare parameters as a pydantic model (the TypeBox analogue); ``execute`` is an
4	async callable ``(tool_call_id, params, signal, on_update) -> ToolResult``. Validation
5	errors and thrown exceptions become tool-error results fed back to the model so it can
6	retry (matching PI).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable
12	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
13	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Literal
14	
15	from pydantic import BaseModel
16	
17	from minima_harness.ai.types import ContentBlock, TextContent, ToolCall
18	
19	if TYPE_CHECKING:
20	    from minima_harness.agent.state import AgentState
21	
22	ToolExecutionMode = Literal["parallel", "sequential"]
23	ThinkingLevel = Literal["off", "minimal", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"]
24	
25	# on_update(partial_result) -> None; called mid-execution for streaming progress.
26	ToolUpdate = Callable[[Any], None]
27	ToolExecute = Callable[[str, BaseModel, object | None, ToolUpdate | None], Awaitable["ToolResult"]]
28	
29	
30	@dataclass(slots=True)
31	class ToolResult:
32	    """What a tool returns. ``content`` goes to the model; ``details`` are app-facing."""
33	
34	    content: list[ContentBlock]
35	    details: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
36	    # Hint to skip the automatic follow-up LLM call. Only honoured when every finalized
37	    # tool result in the batch also sets terminate=True.
38	    terminate: bool = False
39	
40	
41	@dataclass(slots=True)
42	class AgentTool:
43	    name: str
44	    description: str
45	    parameters: type[BaseModel]
46	    execute: ToolExecute
47	    execution_mode: ToolExecutionMode | None = None
48	    label: str = ""
49	
50	
51	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	# Hook types
53	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	
55	
56	@dataclass(slots=True)
57	class BeforeToolCallContext:
58	    tool_call: ToolCall
59	    args: BaseModel  # validated params
60	    context: AgentState
61	
62	
63	@dataclass(slots=True)
64	class BeforeToolCallResult:
65	    block: bool = False
66	    reason: str = ""
67	
68	
69	@dataclass(slots=True)
70	class AfterToolCallContext:
71	    tool_call: ToolCall
72	    result: ToolResult
73	    is_error: bool
74	    context: AgentState
75	
76	
77	@dataclass(slots=True)
78	class AfterToolCallResult:
79	    terminate: bool = False
80	    details: dict[str, Any] | None = None
81	    content: list[ContentBlock] | None = None
82	
83	
84	BeforeToolCall = Callable[[BeforeToolCallContext], Awaitable[BeforeToolCallResult | None]]
85	AfterToolCall = Callable[[AfterToolCallContext], Awaitable[AfterToolCallResult | None]]
86	
87	
88	def find_agent_tool(tools: list[AgentTool], name: str) -> AgentTool | None:
89	    for t in tools:
90	        if t.name == name:
91	            return t
92	    return None
93	
94	
95	def error_result(message: str) -> ToolResult:
96	    """A standard error tool result (single text block)."""
97	    return ToolResult(content=[TextContent(text=message)])
98

10	class WebSearchParams(BaseModel):
11	    query: str = Field(description="The search query.")
12	    num_results: int = Field(default=5, ge=1, le=10, description="How many results to return.")
13	
14	
15	async def _execute(
16	    tool_call_id: str,
17	    params,  # noqa: ANN001
18	    signal,  # noqa: ANN001
19	    on_update: ToolUpdate | None,
20	) -> ToolResult:
21	    assert isinstance(params, WebSearchParams)
22	    try:
23	        data = await exa_search(params.query, params.num_results)
24	    except ExaError as exc:  # auth / transient / bad-response all surface here
25	        return error_result(f"web_search failed: {exc}")
26	
27	    if not data.results:
28	        return ToolResult(content=[TextContent(text="No results found.")], details={"count": 0})
29	
30	    lines: list[str] = []
31	    for i, r in enumerate(data.results, 1):
32	        title = r.title or "(no title)"
33	        date = f" ({r.published_date})" if r.published_date else ""
34	        lines.append(f"[{i}] {title}{date}\n    {r.url}")
35	    body = "\n".join(lines)
36	    return ToolResult(content=[TextContent(text=body)], details={"count": len(data.results)})

45	            "know or that may have changed. To read a result, pass its URL to web_fetch."
46	        ),

[tool_result] toolu_01333afXuhBo29dAf4XW2C7B
1	"""The ``tasks`` tool — the agent's live goal checklist (like Claude Code's TodoWrite).
2	
3	Bound to the session's :class:`~minima_harness.minima.goals.GoalStore` via :func:`tasks_tool`.
4	The model calls it to lay out a plan (``set``) and tick items off (``update``) as it works. The
5	harness re-injects the list into the system prompt each turn so it stays the "north star".
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from typing import Literal
11	
12	from pydantic import BaseModel
13	
14	from minima_harness.agent.tools import AgentTool, ToolResult, error_result
15	from minima_harness.ai.types import TextContent
16	from minima_harness.minima.goals import GoalStore
17	
18	_MARK = {"completed": "[x]", "in_progress": "[~]", "blocked": "[!]", "pending": "[ ]"}
19	
20	
21	class TaskItem(BaseModel):
22	    content: str  # imperative: "Add OAuth login"
23	    active_form: str = ""  # present-continuous: "Adding OAuth login"
24	    status: str = "pending"  # pending | in_progress | completed | blocked
25	
26	
27	class TasksParams(BaseModel):
28	    op: Literal["set", "update", "list"]
29	    tasks: list[TaskItem] | None = None  # op=set: replace the whole list
30	    task_id: str | None = None  # op=update: which task
31	    status: str | None = None  # op=update: new status
32	
33	
34	def _render(store: GoalStore) -> str:
35	    goal = store.goal
36	    if goal is None or not goal.tasks:
37	        return "(no tasks)"
38	    done, total = goal.progress()
39	    head = f"{done}/{total} done" + (f" · {goal.title}" if goal.title else "")
40	    lines = [head] + [f"  {_MARK.get(t.status, '[ ]')} {t.id}  {t.content}" for t in goal.tasks]
41	    return "\n".join(lines)
42	
43	
44	def tasks_tool(store: GoalStore) -> AgentTool:
45	    async def _execute(tool_call_id: str, params, signal, on_update) -> ToolResult:  # noqa: ANN001
46	        assert isinstance(params, TasksParams)
47	        if params.op == "set":
48	            store.set_tasks([t.model_dump() for t in (params.tasks or [])])
49	        elif params.op == "update":
50	            if not params.task_id or not params.status:
51	                return error_result("tasks update needs task_id and status")
52	            if not store.update_task(params.task_id, params.status):
53	                return error_result(
54	                    f"no task {params.task_id!r} (or bad status); use op=list to see ids"
55	                )
56	        # op=list falls through to render
57	        done, total = store.goal.progress() if store.goal else (0, 0)
58	        return ToolResult(
59	            content=[TextContent(text=_render(store))],
60	            details={"completed": done, "total": total},
61	        )
62	
63	    return AgentTool(
64	        name="tasks",
65	        description=(
66	            "Maintain the goal checklist. Use it for multi-step work (3+ steps); skip it for "
67	            "trivial or single-step requests. op='set' replaces the whole list (each task: "
68	            "content imperative + optional active_form); op='update' sets one task's status by "
69	            "task_id (pending|in_progress|completed|blocked); op='list' shows the current list. "
70	            "Keep exactly ONE task in_progress at a time, and mark a task completed ONLY when it "
71	            "is actually done and verified — never on intent."
72	        ),
73	        parameters=TasksParams,
74	        execute=_execute,
75	    )
76

19	class Stream:
20	    """Async iterator over events with a ``.result()`` helper for the final message."""
21	
22	    def __init__(self, gen: AsyncIterator[Event]) -> None:
23	        self._gen = gen
24	        self._result: AssistantMessage | None = None
25	        self._consumed = False
26	
27	    def __aiter__(self) -> Stream:
28	        return self
29	
30	    async def __anext__(self) -> Event:
31	        try:
32	            event = await self._gen.__anext__()
33	        except StopAsyncIteration as exc:
34	            self._consumed = True
35	            raise exc
36	        if isinstance(event, DoneEvent):
37	            self._result = event.message
38	            self._consumed = True
39	        elif isinstance(event, ErrorEvent):
40	            self._result = event.error
41	            self._consumed = True
42	        return event
43	
44	    async def result(self) -> AssistantMessage:
45	        """Drain the stream and return the final assistant message (done or error)."""
46	        async for _ in self:
47	            pass
48	        if self._result is None:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
49	            raise RuntimeError("stream ended without a done/error event")
50	        return self._result

12	def resolve_api_key(options: dict[str, Any] | None, *env_vars: str) -> str | None:
13	    """Options value wins, then the first set environment variable."""
14	    if options and options.get("api_key"):
15	        return str(options["api_key"])
16	    for var in env_vars:
17	        value = os.environ.get(var)
18	        if value:
19	            return value
20	    return None

23	def to_json_schema(model_cls: type[BaseModel]) -> dict[str, Any]:
24	    """A provider-agnostic JSON Schema for a pydantic parameter model.
25	
26	    Strips pydantic-only ``title`` noise and rewrites ``anyOf``/``const`` enum patterns
27	    (from ``Literal``) to plain ``enum`` lists so Google's constrained schema dialect
28	    accepts them.
29	    """
30	    schema = model_cls.model_json_schema()
31	    schema.pop("title", None)
32	    _clean_schema(schema)
33	    return schema
34	
35	
36	def _clean_schema(node: Any) -> None:
37	    if isinstance(node, dict):
38	        node.pop("title", None)
39	        # Literal -> pydantic emits {"anyOf":[{"const": v}, ...]}; flatten to {"enum":[...]}.
40	        any_of = node.get("anyOf")
41	        if isinstance(any_of, list) and all(isinstance(a, dict) and "const" in a for a in any_of):
42	            node["enum"] = [a["const"] for a in any_of]
43	            node.pop("anyOf", None)
44	        for value in node.values():
45	            _clean_schema(value)
46	    elif isinstance(node, list):
47	        for item in node:
48	            _clean_schema(item)

async def _execute_tool_calls(
    tool_calls: list[Any],
    config: AgentLoopConfig,
    state: AgentState,
    out_results: list[tuple[Any, ToolResult, bool]],
) -> AsyncIterator[Any]:
    """Preflight (yield tool_execution_start) then execute; yield updates + ends.

    Appends results to ``out_results`` in assistant SOURCE order; buffered events are
    replayed in completion order.
    """
    plan: list[_PendingTool] = []
    for tc in tool_calls:
        state.pending_tool_calls.add(tc.id)
        tool = find_agent_tool(state.tools, tc.name)
        if tool is None:
            yield ToolExecutionStartEvent(tool_call_id=tc.id, tool_name=tc.name, args=None)
            res = error_result(f"Unknown tool: {tc.name}")
            yield ToolExecutionEndEvent(tool_call_id=tc.id, result=res, is_error=True)
            out_results.append((tc, res, True))
            state.pending_tool_calls.discard(tc.id)
            continue
        try:
            params = tool.parameters.model_validate(tc.arguments)
        except ValidationError as exc:
            yield ToolExecutionStartEvent(tool_call_id=tc.id, tool_name=tc.name, args=None)
            res = error_result(_format_validation_error(exc))
            yield ToolExecutionEndEvent(tool_call_id=tc.id, result=res, is_error=True)
            out_results.append((tc, res, True))
            state.pending_tool_calls.discard(tc.id)
            continue

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import asyncio
4	from pathlib import Path
5	
6	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
7	
8	from minima_harness.agent.tools import AgentTool, ToolResult, ToolUpdate, error_result
9	from minima_harness.ai.types import TextContent
10	
11	
12	class BashParams(BaseModel):
13	    command: str
14	    timeout: int = Field(default=120_000, ge=1)  # milliseconds
15	    workdir: str | None = None
16	
17	
18	async def _execute(
19	    tool_call_id: str,
20	    params,
21	    signal,
22	    on_update: ToolUpdate | None,  # noqa: ANN001
23	) -> ToolResult:
24	    assert isinstance(params, BashParams)
25	    wd = str(Path(params.workdir).expanduser()) if params.workdir else None
26	    try:
27	        proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_shell(
28	            params.command,
29	            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
30	            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.STDOUT,
31	            cwd=wd,
32	            start_new_session=True,
33	        )
34	    except OSError as exc:
35	        return error_result(f"bash: failed to start: {exc}")
36	
37	    chunks: list[str] = []
38	    assert proc.stdout is not None
39	    try:
40	        async with asyncio.timeout(params.timeout / 1000.0):
41	            async for raw in proc.stdout:
42	                chunk = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
43	                chunks.append(chunk)
44	                if on_update is not None:
45	                    try:
46	                        on_update(chunk)
47	                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - progress must never break the run
48	                        pass
49	            await proc.wait()
50	    except TimeoutError:
51	        proc.kill()
52	        await proc.wait()
53	        return error_result(f"bash: timed out after {params.timeout} ms")
54	
55	    output = "".join(chunks)
56	    code = proc.returncode if proc.returncode is not None else -1
57	    body = f"{output}\n[exit {code}]" if output else f"[exit {code}]"
58	    return ToolResult(content=[TextContent(text=body)], details={"exit_code": code})

91	    drag-select can't work there and capturing the mouse would only suppress its rock-solid native
92	    selection. Default OFF there so users can select+copy out of the box."""
93	    if flag is not None:

98	def _tools_for(args: argparse.Namespace):
99	    from minima_harness.tools import default_toolset
100	
101	    tools = [] if args.no_tools else default_toolset()
102	    if args.tools:
103	        allow = {t.strip() for t in args.tools.split(",")}
104	        tools = [t for t in tools if t.name in allow]
105	    if args.exclude_tools:
106	        deny = {t.strip() for t in args.exclude_tools.split(",")}
107	        tools = [t for t in tools if t.name not in deny]
108	    return tools

111	def _register_providers(cwd: Path) -> None:
112	    from minima_harness.ai.provider_catalog import provider_key_present, register_catalog_models
113	    from minima_harness.ai.providers import ensure_providers_registered
114	    from minima_harness.tui.extra_models import register_extra_models
115	
116	    ensure_providers_registered()
117	    # Register the curated multi-provider catalog, but only for providers whose key is
118	    # configured — so the model picker stays relevant (you see models you can actually run).
119	    register_catalog_models()
120	    # OpenRouter is an aggregator: one key unlocks its *entire* live model list (cached +
121	    # offline-safe), not just a few curated ids. Register it when the key is present.
122	    if provider_key_present("openrouter"):
123	        try:
124	            from minima_harness.ai.openrouter_catalog import register_openrouter_models
125	
126	            register_openrouter_models()
127	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never block startup on the OpenRouter catalog
128	            pass
129	    register_extra_models(cwd)

132	def _overlay_minima_prices(config: HarnessConfig) -> None:
133	    """Best-effort: overlay Minima's authoritative live pricing onto the registered models.
134	
135	    So the cost the harness reports for a call matches the cost the server routed against
136	    (keeps est-vs-actual honest). Offline-safe and quick: skipped without a Minima URL, short
137	    timeout, and any failure is swallowed (the seeded prices stand)."""
138	    if not (config.minima_url or "").strip():
139	        return
140	    try:
141	        from minima_client import MinimaClient
142	
143	        from minima_harness.minima.mapping import sync_catalog
144	
145	        with MinimaClient(
146	            config.minima_url, config.minima_api_key, timeout=min(config.timeout, 8.0)
147	        ) as client:
148	            sync_catalog(client)
149	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - pricing overlay must never block startup
150	        pass

153	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
154	    _load_env_files()
155	    raw = sys.argv[1:] if argv is None else list(argv)
156	
157	    # `minima-harness config …` — credential setup (no TUI; works before any keys exist).
158	    if raw and raw[0] == "config":
159	        from minima_harness.tui.config_cli import config_cli
160	
161	        return config_cli(raw[1:])
162	
163	    # `minima-harness install|list|remove …` — package management (no TUI).
164	    if raw and raw[0] in _PKG_COMMANDS:
165	        return packages_cli(raw[0], raw[1:])
166	
167	    args = _build_parser().parse_args(raw)
168	    config = HarnessConfig.from_env()
169	    if args.offline:
170	        config.minima_url = ""
171	    cwd = Path.cwd()
172	    _register_providers(cwd)
173	    # Gate the routing candidate pool to models whose provider key is configured (after
174	    # registration so newly-added providers count) — Minima won't be offered a model the
175	    # user can't run. No-op when keys for the defaults (Anthropic/Gemini) are present.
176	    from minima_harness.ai.provider_catalog import runnable_candidates
177	
178	    config.candidates = runnable_candidates(config.candidates)
179	    _overlay_minima_prices(config)
180	    tools = _tools_for(args)
181	
182	    noninteractive = args.print or args.mode in ("print", "json")
183	    if noninteractive:
184	        prompt = " ".join(args.prompt).strip()
185	        if not prompt:
186	            print("minima-harness: --print/--mode json requires a prompt", file=sys.stderr)
187	            return 2
188	        agent = MinimaAgent(
189	            config, tools=tools, meter=CostMeter(), system_prompt=build_system_prompt(cwd)
190	        )
191	        from minima_harness.tui.run_modes import run_json, run_print
192	
193	        runner = run_json if args.mode == "json" else run_print
194	        return asyncio.run(runner(agent, prompt))
195	
196	    mgr = SessionManager()
197	    load_on_start = False
198	    try:
199	        if args.no_session:
200	            session = SessionStore.in_memory()
201	        elif args.session or args.continue_last:
202	            session = mgr.open(cwd, session_id=args.session)
203	            load_on_start = True
204	        else:
205	            session = mgr.new(cwd, name=args.name)
206	        if args.name:
207	            session.display_name = args.name
208	    except FileNotFoundError as exc:
209	        print(f"minima-harness: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
210	        return 2
211	
212	    app = HarnessApp(
213	        config,
214	        session=session,
215	        tools=tools,
216	        cwd=cwd,
217	        system_prompt=build_system_prompt(cwd),
218	        load_session=load_on_start,
219	        skip_permissions=args.dangerously_skip_permissions,
220	        mouse=(mouse := _resolve_mouse(args.mouse)),
221	    )

18	def event_to_dict(event) -> dict:  # noqa: ANN001
19	    """Serialize an AgentEvent into a JSON-friendly dict (PI-style JSON mode)."""
20	    if isinstance(event, MessageUpdateEvent):
21	        stream = event.assistant_message_event
22	        if isinstance(stream, TextDeltaEvent):
23	            return {"type": "text_delta", "delta": stream.delta}
24	        if isinstance(stream, ErrorEvent):
25	            err = stream.error
26	            return {
27	                "type": "error",
28	                "message": getattr(err, "error_message", "") or "provider error",
29	                "model": getattr(err, "model", ""),
30	            }
31	        return {"type": "message_update"}
32	    if isinstance(event, ToolExecutionStartEvent):
33	        return {"type": "tool_start", "name": event.tool_name}
34	    if isinstance(event, ToolExecutionEndEvent):
35	        return {"type": "tool_end", "is_error": event.is_error}
36	    if isinstance(event, TurnEndEvent):
37	        return {"type": "turn_end"}
38	    if isinstance(event, AgentEndEvent):
39	        return {"type": "done"}
40	    if isinstance(event, AgentStartEvent):
41	        return {"type": "start"}
42	    return {"type": getattr(event, "type", "unknown")}

45	async def run_print(agent: MinimaAgent, prompt: str) -> int:
46	    """One-shot: run the prompt, print the final assistant text, exit.
47	
48	    A provider failure (bad key, 404, network) produces empty output; report the classified
49	    reason on stderr and exit non-zero instead of silently printing a blank line.
50	    """
51	    await agent.prompt(prompt)
52	    err = getattr(agent, "_last_error", None)
53	    last = agent._last_assistant()  # noqa: SLF001
54	    text = last.text if last is not None else ""
55	    if err and not text.strip():
56	        print(err, file=sys.stderr)
57	        return 1
58	    print(text)
59	    return 0
60	
61	
62	async def run_json(agent: MinimaAgent, prompt: str) -> int:
63	    """Stream every AgentEvent as a JSON line, then exit."""
64	    agent.subscribe(lambda event: print(json.dumps(event_to_dict(event)), flush=True))
65	    await agent.prompt(prompt)
66	    return 0

116	        slider: float | None = None,
117	        files: list[str | Path] | None = None,
118	        tags: list[str] | None = None,
119	    ) -> RoutingResult | None:
120	        task_text = _text_of(content)
121	        effective_task_type = task_type or self._task_type_hint
122	        self._prompts_run += 1
123	        self._rejected_tools = 0  # reset per-turn reject tally
124	        self._last_error = None  # reset per-turn error
125	        self._last_error_raw = None
126	        self._reroute_note = None  # reset per-turn auto-reroute note
127	
128	        routing: RoutingResult | None = None
129	        last: AssistantMessage | None = None
130	        run_error: BaseException | None = None
131	        latency_ms = 0
132	        turns_taken = 0
133	        # Snapshot history so a failed turn can be rolled back out of the agent's context entirely.
134	        msgs_before = len(self.state.messages)
135	        # A hard auth failure (bad/invalid/missing key) is deterministic — the same provider fails
136	        # identically on every call. So when one occurs and a *different* provider's key works,
137	        # blacklist the dead provider for the session and re-run the SAME message on an alternative,
138	        # rescuing this turn instead of wasting it. The exclusion set grows by one per pass, so the
139	        # loop always terminates; range() is a hard backstop.
140	        for _attempt in range(self._reroute_budget() + 1):
141	            routing = await self._route(
142	                task_text, effective_task_type, slider, files=files, tags=tags, reroute=_attempt > 0
143	            )
144	            # On a RErouTE pass: if routing handed back a provider already blacklisted this turn,
145	            # re-running it would just fail identically — stop and surface the prior error. This
146	            # catches the cases the candidate filter can't: an offline route (which can't switch
147	            # models) and a recommender that ignores the candidate constraint and re-picks the dead
148	            # model. Gated to reroute passes so the FIRST attempt always runs (and surfaces a real
149	            # error) even when the only model's provider was excluded on a previous turn.
150	            run_provider = _provider_of(self.state.model.id if self.state.model else None)
151	            already_dead = run_provider is not None and run_provider in self._excluded_providers
152	            if _attempt > 0 and already_dead:
153	                break
154	            msgs_before = len(self.state.messages)
155	            start = monotonic()
156	            run_error = None
157	            try:
158	                await super().prompt(content)
159	            except BaseException as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - capture, then re-raise after feedback
160	                run_error = exc
161	            latency_ms = int((monotonic() - start) * 1000)
162	            turns_taken = self.state.turns_taken
163	            last = self._last_assistant()
164	            # A provider call that failed (bad/missing key, 404, network) is swallowed by the
165	            # provider into an empty-text assistant with stop_reason="error" — NOT a raised
166	            # exception. Treat that as a failed turn so (a) Minima is never told a broken turn
167	            # "succeeded" (which would poison routing), and (b) the caller can surface why.
168	            provider_error = last is not None and getattr(last, "stop_reason", None) == "error"
169	            if provider_error and last is not None:
170	                self._last_error = classify_provider_error(last.error_message, last.model)
171	                self._last_error_raw = last.error_message
172	                # Log the raw provider error so it's recoverable even off the TUI (--print).
173	                _log.warning("provider_error_raw model=%s: %s", last.model, last.error_message)
174	            # Auto-reroute off a dead-key provider — but never second-guess an explicit pin.
175	            if (
176	                provider_error
177	                and run_error is None
178	                and last is not None
179	                and not self.config.pinned
180	                and is_auth_error(last.error_message)
181	            ):
182	                provider = _provider_of(last.model)
183	                if provider:
184	                    self._excluded_providers.add(provider)
185	                    if self._has_runnable_candidate():
186	                        self._note_reroute(provider)
187	                        del self.state.messages[msgs_before:]  # drop failed attempt, then retry
188	                        self._last_error = self._last_error_raw = None
189	                        continue
190	            break
191	
192	        provider_error = last is not None and getattr(last, "stop_reason", None) == "error"
193	        if provider_error and last is not None:
194	            # The final loop pass may have cleared these on a reroute `continue` that then couldn't
195	            # actually switch providers (offline, or a recommender that re-picked the dead model).
196	            # Re-derive so a still-failing turn surfaces the real error (not a blank "success") and
197	            # drop the optimistic reroute note (the reroute did NOT rescue the turn).
198	            self._last_error = classify_provider_error(last.error_message, last.model)
199	            self._last_error_raw = last.error_message
200	            self._reroute_note = None
201	        # An auth/infra failure is a credential problem, not a model-quality signal — don't feed it
202	        # back to Minima (it would poison the model's success estimate in this namespace).
203	        auth_failed = bool(
204	            provider_error and last is not None and is_auth_error(last.error_message)
205	        )
206	        failed = run_error is not None or provider_error
207	        quality: float | None = None
208	        outcome = "success"
209	        if routing is not None and not auth_failed:
210	            quality, outcome = await self._feedback_safely(
211	                task_text, routing, latency_ms, failed, turns_taken
212	            )
213	        if self.meter is not None:
214	            actual = last.usage.cost.total if last is not None else 0.0
215	            self.meter.record(
216	                label=_short_label(task_text),
217	                routing=routing,
218	                actual_cost_usd=actual,
219	                quality=quality if not failed else 0.0,
220	                outcome=("failure" if failed else outcome),
221	                turns=turns_taken,
222	            )
223	        # Roll a failed turn fully out of the agent's context — both the empty error-assistant
224	        # AND the user message that triggered it. A failed turn produced no usable exchange, so
225	        # leaving it in history only poisons the NEXT turn (the loop's _drop_failed_calls guard
226	        # already strips the empty assistant; this also avoids a dangling user turn). Done after
227	        # feedback/meter so they still see the failed turn's signal.
228	        if failed:
229	            del self.state.messages[msgs_before:]
230	        if run_error is not None:
231	            raise run_error
232	        return routing
